questions.question: Why did prior sparse-attention approaches fail to stick?
options: Fixed patterns (sliding/strided/block) throw away information and fail long-range recall; inference-time sparsity (H2O, StreamingLLM) prunes a model pre-trained on DENSE attention, recovering only a fraction of the speedup since the model never learned to route through the sparse pattern
questions.explanation: At 64k tokens attention eats 70-80% of decode latency — NSA (ACL 2025 best paper) is the fix that stuck because it's both natively trainable AND kernel-aligned.

questions.question: What are NSA's three parallel branches?
options: Compressed (blocks of ~32-64 tokens summarized by a learned MLP for a coarse whole-sequence view), selected (top-k most relevant blocks read at full resolution, routed by compressed-branch scores), and sliding window (~512 recent tokens for local syntax) — combined by a learned per-position gate
questions.explanation: out = g_cmp·out_cmp + g_sel·out_sel + g_win·out_win, with gates from a small MLP on the query that don't need to sum to 1.

questions.question: How does NSA make discrete top-k selection trainable?
options: The top-k is a forward-graph NO-OP that only controls which blocks load from memory — gradients flow through the compressed-branch attention scores, which influence both the compressed output AND the selection logic, plus through the selected blocks' contribution
By annealing k from N down to 16
questions.explanation: That's the "natively trainable" trick: the differentiable coarse attention doubles as the routing signal, so pre-training shapes the sparsity pattern end to end.

questions.question: What makes NSA's kernel "hardware-aligned"?
options: Queries load by GQA group with selection shared per-group (not per-head), so sparse KV block fetches amortize across the group and attention runs in SRAM — arithmetic intensity stays high instead of degrading into scattered memory reads
It only runs on Hopper GPUs
questions.explanation: Sparsity that ignores the memory hierarchy delivers paper FLOP savings but no wall-clock win; NSA beats FlashAttention on 64k decode while matching or beating full-attention quality.

questions.question: Why does each branch exist — what would break with only two?
options: Compressed alone is too coarse for exact recall; selection needs the compressed scores as its routing signal; and the sliding window covers structure-heavy short-range patterns (syntax, local coreference) the global branches would miss
questions.explanation: Coarse overview + targeted fine detail + local context — three views of the same KV cache, each cheap, jointly covering what full attention provided expensively.
